title: Massive Meaty Vietnamese Beef Bun Rieu
description: A hearty, comforting Vietnamese beef noodle soup featuring fermented sausage, sweet red peppers, and a rich, meaty broth.
instructions: Combine fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and ground black pepper in a small bowl.
Heat 1 tablespoon of cooking oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, ginger, and garlic to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the fermented sausage to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the red bell pepper to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is tender, about 5 minutes.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes.
Season the broth with salt to taste.
Cook the Bun Rieu noodles according to the package instructions.
Add the cooked noodles to the pot and stir to combine.
Drizzle the hoisin sauce and sesame oil over the top of the noodles and stir to combine.
Sprinkle the sesame seeds over the top of the noodles and serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
